title: tilia americana var. pubescens
intro: Tilia americana var. pubescens, also known as the American basswood or hairy American basswood, is a deciduous tree native to eastern North America. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and sedative properties. The medicinal properties of Tilia americana var. pubescens are believed to help with anxiety, insomnia, and stress relief, as well as providing relief from headaches and fever. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote relaxation and improve overall well-being.
